task:
ATOM 0 — Owner map for the Drift Audit reframe (docs only; NO product-code edits). Produce a new doc at `docs/drift-audit-ownermap.md`, mirroring the structure and rigor of the existing `docs/onboarding-rebuild-ownermap.md` (read that file first as the template — match its sections, table shape, and evidence style).

GOAL: Pin exactly what is REUSE-AS-IS vs ADAPT vs NEW before any Drift engine work begins, so subsequent atoms (P1 read-claims, P2 read-reality, P3 compare, P4 report, P5/P6 ratify+apply) build on a confirmed reuse boundary rather than a guess.

WHAT TO PRODUCE — the doc must contain:
1. A one-paragraph framing: how the Drift Audit runs as an ORDINARY Oscar-driven priority (atoms call engines directly as library tooling; founder gates = ordinary wrap/verify beats, NOT an in-loop phase-executor). Cite ADR-0026 (the reframe that retires the executor/runner-mode) and ADR-0020 (the three onboarding situations; Drift is the third). Confirm the `cocoder/**`-only audit write boundary (ADR-0023) applies to the apply step only.
2. A classification table with one row per relevant onboard-existing engine/symbol. Columns: Engine/symbol | File:line (concrete, verified by opening the file) | Drift phase it serves | Verdict (REUSE-AS-IS / ADAPT / NEW) | Note (if ADAPT: what changes; if NEW: why no existing owner). At minimum cover the symbols named in the priority's Reuse map: P2 read-reality — `inventoryRepo`, `runAgenticRecon` (recon/recon-pass), deep-read fan-out `runDeepReadSource`/`combineSourcePair` (p2-fanout), `resolveDeepReadAssignments`/`createDeepReadTurn` (p2-dispatch); P5/P6 — `p6-apply` materialize-staged-to-`cocoder/**`, `AuditWriteBoundary`/`AuditWriteBoundaryError` at `runCommitGate`; driver+gates — the ordinary Oscar↔Bob run loop + directive/verify/wrap/resume. VERIFY each file:line by actually opening the source — do not copy line numbers from the priority text.
3. The three genuinely-NEW pieces called out explicitly with where they should live and their input→output contract: P1 read-claims (pure deterministic reader of target `cocoder/` governance → claims inventory; refuse-on-malformed), P3 compare (claims-vs-reality diff → traceable drift findings; empty inputs → empty output; non-gameable), P4 report (drift report + amendment/ticket drafts as ARTIFACTS only; never rewrites governance in place).
4. A short 'open seams / risks' section: anything that looks like REUSE but might actually need ADAPT, and any symbol named in the priority that you could NOT locate (flag it explicitly rather than inventing a line number).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (a) `docs/drift-audit-ownermap.md` exists and follows the onboarding-rebuild-ownermap template; (b) every file:line in the table was verified by opening the actual source file (no fabricated locations); (c) every symbol from the priority's Reuse map appears with a verdict, or is explicitly listed as not-found in the risks section; (d) the three NEW pieces each have a stated location + input→output contract; (e) NO edits to any product code or `cocoder/**` — the ONLY new/changed file is `docs/drift-audit-ownermap.md`.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: do not touch product code, personas, or `cocoder/**`. This is a read-and-document atom only. Report the exact path written and a 3-5 line summary of the REUSE/ADAPT/NEW split.

ATOM 1 — Build the NEW P1 read-claims engine for the Drift Audit (the first genuinely-new piece per docs/drift-audit-ownermap.md). Read that owner map first; it sets the contract.

DELIVERABLE: a pure, deterministic governance reader as LIBRARY tooling only — NO runner/loop wiring this atom (that is a later atom).
- New file `packages/core/src/drift/read-claims.ts`.
- New barrel `packages/core/src/drift/index.ts` re-exporting the public symbols.
- Re-export the public types + the reader function from the core barrel `packages/core/src/index.ts` (follow the existing `export {…} from './…/index.js'` style — note the `.js` extensions).
- New test `packages/core/tests/drift-read-claims.test.ts` (vitest; mirror the tmpdir-fixture style of `packages/core/tests/playbook-recon.test.ts`).

CONTRACT (model on `inventoryRepo` in packages/core/src/playbooks/recon.ts — pure, fs-read-only, fully-typed `readonly` output, deterministic):
- Signature roughly: `readGovernanceClaims(opts: { repoRoot: string; cocoderDir?: string }): DriftClaimsInventory` where cocoderDir defaults to `<repoRoot>/cocoder`.
- Output `DriftClaimsInventory`: a `readonly` typed object holding a stable-sorted `readonly claims: DriftClaim[]`, plus a small summary (counts by category). Each `DriftClaim` carries: a stable deterministic `id`, a `category`, the human-readable `claim` text/label it asserts, and REQUIRED `evidence` = { file (repo-relative path), line (1-based) } anchoring it to the governance source. A claim with no source evidence must never be emitted.
- Categories to extract DETERMINISTICALLY from machine-readable governance structure (NOT via any LLM/agentic call — this engine is pure and offline):
  * ADR claims — one per `cocoder/decisions/NNNN-*.md`: id + status (parse the 'Status:'/frontmatter status, e.g. Accepted/Superseded/Proposed) + title, anchored to the file:line where each is declared.
  * Priority claims — one per `cocoder/priorities/*.md`: frontmatter id/title and any declared `scopeNarrowing`/`auditWriteBoundary`, anchored to the frontmatter line.
  * Codebase-map / memory claims — from `cocoder/memory/codebase-map.md` (and tech-stack.md): each asserted subsystem→path mapping or named path reference, anchored to its line.
  * Standards/scope claims — declared scopes/headings under `cocoder/standards/**` and PLAYBOOK.md as available.
  Pick a crisp, parseable rule per category; keep extraction mechanical and reproducible. Do NOT attempt fuzzy natural-language claim mining.

HARD INVARIANTS (these are the acceptance gate):
- PURE + DETERMINISTIC: no writes anywhere; same governance input → byte-identical output; stable sort of claims (e.g. by category then id then file:line). No Date.now()/Math.random()/network/agentic calls.
- REFUSE-ON-MALFORMED: throw a clear, specific Error (path + reason in the message) on: a required governance file that is unreadable, a duplicate claim id, or any malformed required structure. Distinguish 'required' from 'optional': a missing OPTIONAL governance category (e.g. no standards dir, empty codebase-map) yields an EMPTY claim set for that category, NOT a throw. Empty/absent governance overall → an inventory with zero claims (never invented claims).
- EVIDENCE-BACKED: every emitted claim has a real file:line; no fabricated anchors.

TESTS (must cover, all green): (1) a representative tmpdir governance fixture → expected claims with correct file:line anchors across >=2 categories; (2) refuse-on-malformed — duplicate id or unreadable required file throws with a useful message; (3) empty/minimal governance → zero claims (no throw, no invented claims); (4) determinism — calling twice yields deeply-equal output.

MUST NOT BREAK / SCOPE GUARD: write ONLY under `packages/core/src/drift/**`, the one `packages/core/src/index.ts` barrel export line(s), and the new test file. Do NOT edit existing playbook engines, the runner, personas, or anything under `cocoder/**`. Do NOT wire this into the run loop.

VERIFY EVIDENCE I WILL CHECK: the drift test passes (`pnpm --dir 'packages/core' exec vitest run drift-read-claims`), the core typecheck passes, and the whole-tree diff touches only the allowed paths. Report the exact command outputs (exit codes), the files changed, and a 4-6 line summary of the claim categories + the refuse-on-malformed behavior.

ATOM 2 — Build the deterministic P2 read-reality inventory for the Drift Audit (the reality baseline P3 compare will diff P1 claims against). Read docs/drift-audit-ownermap.md first; this atom delivers ONLY the deterministic reality view. The agentic deep-read fan-out reuse (runAgenticRecon/runDeepReadSource) and the createDeepReadTurn artifact-path ADAPT are DEFERRED to a later atom — do NOT touch them here.

DELIVERABLE — a pure, deterministic reality reader as LIBRARY tooling, alongside the P1 engine:
- New file `packages/core/src/drift/read-reality.ts`.
- Export its public symbols through the existing `packages/core/src/drift/index.ts` barrel, and onward through `packages/core/src/index.ts` (match the `.js`-extension re-export style already there).
- New test `packages/core/tests/drift-read-reality.test.ts` (vitest; tmpdir-fixture style like `packages/core/tests/playbook-recon.test.ts` and `packages/core/tests/drift-read-claims.test.ts`).

CONTRACT:
- Signature roughly: `readRepoReality(opts: { repoRoot: string }): DriftRealityInventory`.
- REUSE `inventoryRepo` from `packages/core/src/playbooks/recon.ts` AS-IS for the structured repo facts (packages, scripts, entry points, roots, frameworks). Do NOT fork, copy, or edit `inventoryRepo` or recon.ts — import and wrap it.
- Output `DriftRealityInventory`: a `readonly` typed object holding (a) the `RepoInventory` from `inventoryRepo`, and (b) a `paths` view that lets P3 deterministically resolve whether a governance-referenced path EXISTS — i.e. a stable-sorted `readonly` set/list of existing repo-relative paths (files and directories), normalized with `/` separators. This is the key new primitive: P1 memory/priority claims reference paths, and P3 must answer 'does this path still exist in reality?'. Use the SAME ignore conventions recon uses (skip `.git`, `node_modules`, `dist`, `out`, `build`, `coverage`, `.next`, `.turbo`) — if recon exposes a reusable walker/ignore set, reuse it; otherwise a minimal local walk that mirrors those ignores is acceptable, but keep it small and do not duplicate inventoryRepo's parsing logic.
- Optionally include a small `summary` (e.g. counts: total paths, file vs dir) for cheap diagnostics. Keep the output pure DATA (serializable) — no closures/resolver functions in the returned object; P3 will build its own lookup from the path list.

HARD INVARIANTS (the acceptance gate):
- PURE + DETERMINISTIC: fs-read-only, no writes, no Date.now()/Math.random()/network/agentic/LLM calls; same repo tree → byte-identical output; stable sort of the path list.
- Empty/sparse repo → a valid inventory with an empty-or-minimal path set (never a throw for an ordinary empty dir). A genuinely unreadable repoRoot may throw with a clear message.
- The returned `paths` must contain real, existing repo-relative paths only (resolvable on disk) — no invented entries; and it must include the paths P3 needs to detect stale references (e.g. a referenced source file that exists shows up; one that was deleted does not).

TESTS (must cover, all green): (1) a representative tmpdir repo fixture (a couple of packages + a src file or two) → reality includes the expected existing repo-relative paths AND the reused `inventoryRepo` facts (e.g. a detected package/script); (2) a path that does NOT exist is absent from `paths` (the stale-reference signal P3 relies on); (3) ignored dirs (e.g. `node_modules/`) are excluded from `paths`; (4) determinism — two calls deep-equal.

MUST NOT BREAK / SCOPE GUARD: write ONLY under `packages/core/src/drift/**`, the one `packages/core/src/index.ts` barrel export line(s), and the new test file. Do NOT edit recon.ts/inventoryRepo, the deep-read fan-out, createDeepReadTurn, the runner, personas, or anything under `cocoder/**`. Do NOT wire this into the run loop, and do NOT add agentic recon here.

VERIFY EVIDENCE I WILL CHECK: the new test passes (`pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run drift-read-reality`), the FULL core suite still passes (`pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run`), workspace typecheck passes (`pnpm -w run typecheck`), and the whole-tree diff touches only the allowed paths (recon.ts unchanged). Report exact command exit codes, files changed, and a 4-6 line summary of the reality shape + how `paths` supports stale-reference detection.

ATOM 3 — Build the NEW P3 compare engine for the Drift Audit: diff P1 claims against P2 reality into traceable, non-gameable drift findings. Read docs/drift-audit-ownermap.md and the two existing engines (packages/core/src/drift/read-claims.ts, read-reality.ts) first.

TWO PARTS, both LIBRARY tooling (no run-loop wiring):

PART A — Make claims structured so compare consumes DATA, not prose (additive, backward-compatible enrichment of P1):
- In `packages/core/src/drift/read-claims.ts`, add an OPTIONAL structured field to `DriftClaim`: `references?: readonly DriftClaimReference[]` where `DriftClaimReference = { readonly kind: 'path' | 'glob'; readonly value: string }`.
- Populate it for the categories that assert resolvable references: `memory` claims get a `{ kind: 'path', value: <the referenced repo-relative path> }` (the same path already in extractPathRefs); `priority` claims with scopeNarrowing get one `{ kind: 'glob', value: <glob> }` per scopeNarrowing entry. (auditWriteBoundary entries need NOT be emitted as references.) Categories without resolvable references simply omit the field.
- This is ADDITIVE: existing `id`/`category`/`claim`/`evidence` fields and their values stay byte-identical. UPDATE `packages/core/tests/drift-read-claims.test.ts` so its `toEqual` expectations include the new `references` field where applicable (do NOT weaken the test — extend it).
- Export `DriftClaimReference` through the drift barrel and core barrel.

PART B — the compare engine:
- New file `packages/core/src/drift/compare.ts`; export public symbols through `packages/core/src/drift/index.ts` and onward through `packages/core/src/index.ts` (`.js`-extension style).
- Signature roughly: `compareDrift(claims: DriftClaimsInventory, reality: DriftRealityInventory): DriftComparison` where `DriftComparison = { readonly version: 1; readonly findings: readonly DriftFinding[]; readonly summary: {...} }`.
- `DriftFinding` REQUIRES concrete evidence from BOTH sides: `{ id (stable), kind, severity, claim: { id, category, text, evidence: {file,line}, reference }, reality: { detail }, suggestedKind }`. `claim.reference` is the asserted path/glob; `reality.detail` is the concrete contradiction (e.g. 'path not found in reality' or 'scope glob matches no existing path'). `suggestedKind` is an amendment/ticket-draft hint for P4 (e.g. 'update-codebase-map' | 'update-priority-scope' | 'ticket') — P3 emits hints ONLY, it writes nothing.
- Deterministic finding kinds for this atom:
  * `stale-path-reference` — a `memory` claim whose `{kind:'path'}` reference is NOT present in `reality.paths` → finding. A memory path that DOES exist in reality.paths → NO finding.
  * `dead-scope-glob` — a `priority` claim whose `{kind:'glob'}` reference matches ZERO entries in `reality.paths` → finding. A glob matching >=1 reality path → NO finding.
- REUSE the existing glob matcher `matchesAny` from `packages/core/src/write-scope/glob.js` for glob matching (read it first); do NOT fork or write a new glob implementation. For path existence, build a Set from `reality.paths` (match against the entry `path` strings).

NON-GAMEABLE INVARIANTS (the acceptance gate):
- A finding is emitted ONLY when there is concrete evidence from BOTH sides (a claim reference AND a proven reality contradiction). No finding may be synthesized from one side alone.
- Empty claims → ZERO findings. Empty `reality.paths` → ZERO findings (cannot fabricate drift from missing reality). Determinism: same inputs → byte-identical output; stable sort of findings (e.g. by kind then claim id).
- No path/glob is re-parsed out of claim free-text — compare consumes the structured `references` only.

TESTS — new `packages/core/tests/drift-compare.test.ts`, all green: (1) memory claim referencing a MISSING path → exactly one `stale-path-reference` finding carrying both claim evidence (file:line) and reality detail; a memory claim referencing an EXISTING path → no finding; (2) priority scope glob matching zero reality paths → one `dead-scope-glob` finding; a glob that matches → no finding; (3) non-gameable — empty claims → 0 findings, AND empty reality.paths → 0 findings; (4) determinism — two calls deep-equal. Prefer feeding compare REAL outputs of readGovernanceClaims/readRepoReality over hand-built fixtures where practical, to prove the contract end-to-end.

MUST NOT BREAK / SCOPE GUARD: write ONLY under `packages/core/src/drift/**` (compare.ts + the additive read-claims.ts enrichment), the `packages/core/src/index.ts` barrel line(s), and the test files (new drift-compare.test.ts + updated drift-read-claims.test.ts). Do NOT edit read-reality.ts, recon.ts, the deep-read fan-out, the runner, personas, write-scope/glob.ts, or anything under `cocoder/**`. Do NOT wire into the run loop.

VERIFY EVIDENCE I WILL CHECK: `pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run drift-compare drift-read-claims` passes, the FULL core suite passes (`pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run`), workspace typecheck passes (`pnpm -w run typecheck`), and the whole-tree diff stays inside the allowed paths (read-reality.ts and recon.ts unchanged). Report exact exit codes, files changed, and a 5-7 line summary of the finding kinds + how the both-sides/empty-input invariants are enforced.

ATOM 4 — Build the NEW P4 report engine for the Drift Audit: turn a P3 DriftComparison into a drift report + amendment/ticket DRAFT artifacts. Read docs/drift-audit-ownermap.md and packages/core/src/drift/compare.ts first.

CRITICAL CONSTRAINT (the heart of this atom): P4 emits ARTIFACTS ONLY as in-memory DATA for the caller to write under the run directory. It MUST NOT write any file, and it MUST NOT rewrite governance in place. No fs writes of any kind inside the engine. The apply step (a later atom) is the only thing that ever lands ratified content in cocoder/**.

DELIVERABLE — pure renderer as LIBRARY tooling (no run-loop wiring):
- New file `packages/core/src/drift/report.ts`; export public symbols through `packages/core/src/drift/index.ts` and onward through `packages/core/src/index.ts` (`.js`-extension re-export style).
- New test `packages/core/tests/drift-report.test.ts` (vitest; tmpdir/style consistent with the other drift tests, but note the engine itself does NO fs — feed it a DriftComparison and assert the returned data).

CONTRACT:
- Signature roughly: `buildDriftReport(comparison: DriftComparison, opts?: { target?: string; generatedAt?: string }): DriftReportPackage`. Any timestamp/label in the report header comes ONLY from opts (passed in by the caller) — the engine generates NO time/randomness itself (no Date.now()/new Date()/Math.random()). If opts.generatedAt is absent, omit the date line entirely rather than inventing one.
- `DriftReportPackage` = readonly { version: 1; report: { relativePath: string /* e.g. 'report.md' */; content: string }; findings: { relativePath: string /* e.g. 'findings.json' */; content: string /* JSON.stringify of the comparison, stable */ }; drafts: readonly DriftDraft[] }.
- `DriftDraft` = readonly { kind: 'amendment' | 'ticket'; findingId: string; targetPath: string /* the implicated cocoder governance file, derived from the finding's claim.evidence.file */; relativePath: string /* e.g. `drafts/<findingId>.md`, RELATIVE, run-dir-anchored */; title: string; content: string /* self-contained markdown citing the concrete mismatch */ }.
- Map each finding to a draft via its `suggestedKind`: 'update-codebase-map' and 'update-priority-scope' => kind 'amendment'; 'ticket' => kind 'ticket'. One draft per finding.
- `report.md` content must, for EACH finding, surface: the kind, severity, the claim evidence (file:line) and asserted reference, and the reality detail — i.e. every report line traces to a concrete both-sides mismatch. Include a short summary header (counts by kind). Every draft's `content` must likewise cite the finding's claim evidence + reality detail so a founder ratifying it sees the concrete justification.

INVARIANTS (the acceptance gate):
- NO FS WRITES, NO IN-PLACE GOVERNANCE EDITS: the engine returns data only; all paths in the package are RELATIVE (run-dir-anchored), never absolute, never under an existing cocoder/** file as a write target. (targetPath names the implicated governance file for founder context, but the engine does not write it.)
- PURE + DETERMINISTIC: same DriftComparison (+ same opts) => byte-identical DriftReportPackage; stable ordering of drafts (follow the comparison's already-sorted findings). No time/random.
- EMPTY-INPUT: a comparison with zero findings => a valid report (state plainly that no drift was found) and an EMPTY drafts array — never a throw, never invented findings/drafts.
- TRACEABILITY: every draft.findingId maps to a real finding; every draft cites that finding's evidence.

TESTS (new drift-report.test.ts, all green): (1) a comparison with >=2 findings of different kinds => report.md mentions each finding's kind + claim file:line + reality detail; drafts has one entry per finding with correct kind mapping ('amendment' vs 'ticket') and targetPath derived from the claim evidence file; all relativePaths are relative; (2) empty comparison => report states no drift and drafts is []; no throw; (3) determinism => two calls with same input deep-equal; (4) purity => calling buildDriftReport creates NO files on disk (e.g. run it with a known tmp cwd and assert nothing new was written) and every package path is relative. Prefer feeding it a REAL compareDrift(...) output where practical.

MUST NOT BREAK / SCOPE GUARD: write ONLY under `packages/core/src/drift/**` (report.ts), the `packages/core/src/index.ts` barrel line(s), and the new test file. Do NOT edit compare.ts/read-claims.ts/read-reality.ts/recon.ts, the runner, personas, or anything under `cocoder/**`. Do NOT wire into the run loop.

VERIFY EVIDENCE I WILL CHECK: `pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run drift-report` passes, the FULL core suite passes (`pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run`), workspace typecheck passes (`pnpm -w run typecheck`), and the whole-tree diff stays inside the allowed paths (the other drift engines unchanged). Report exact exit codes, files changed, and a 5-7 line summary of the report/draft shape + how the no-fs-write and empty-input invariants are enforced.

ATOM 5 — Build the Drift APPLY primitive: the cocoder/**-boundary-enforced materializer that lands a founder-ratified write-set into the working tree with refuse-not-flag. Read docs/drift-audit-ownermap.md, packages/core/src/commit-gate/gate.ts (AuditWriteBoundary, AuditWriteBoundaryError), and packages/core/src/write-scope/partition.ts (partitionByScope) first.

SCOPE OF THIS ATOM (read carefully): this builds ONLY the deterministic apply primitive — given an already-ratified set of concrete writes, materialize them into cocoder/** and REFUSE (not flag) any path outside the boundary. It does NOT decide the P4-draft -> concrete-write mapping, does NOT do in-place governance editing semantics, and does NOT perform the git commit (the existing runCommitGate spine owns the commit-time boundary; this primitive prepares the working tree + provides defense-in-depth pre-validation). The founder ratification itself is a live beat, not built here.

DELIVERABLE — pure-logic + fs-write LIBRARY tooling (no run-loop wiring):
- New file `packages/core/src/drift/apply.ts`; export public symbols through `packages/core/src/drift/index.ts` and onward through `packages/core/src/index.ts` (`.js`-extension re-export style).
- New test `packages/core/tests/drift-apply.test.ts` (vitest; tmpdir repo fixtures like the other drift tests).

CONTRACT:
- Signature roughly: `applyRatifiedDriftWrites(input: { repoRoot: string; boundary: AuditWriteBoundary; writes: readonly DriftWrite[] }): DriftApplyResult` where `DriftWrite = { readonly path: string /* repo-relative target, e.g. 'cocoder/tickets/foo.md' */; readonly content: string }` and `DriftApplyResult = { readonly written: readonly string[] /* repo-relative, stable-sorted */ }`.
- REUSE, do not fork: import `AuditWriteBoundary` and `AuditWriteBoundaryError` from the commit-gate module, and `partitionByScope` from write-scope, to perform the boundary check. The boundary scope for Drift is `['cocoder/**']` (passed in via `boundary`). Do NOT restate the glob/scope rule or define a second boundary type — compose the existing ones.

HARD INVARIANTS (the acceptance gate — this is the trust boundary, ADR-0020 Decision 5 / ADR-0023):
- REFUSE-NOT-FLAG, ALL-OR-NOTHING: pre-validate EVERY write.path against the boundary BEFORE writing anything. If any path is outside `boundary.scope` (use partitionByScope) OR escapes the repo via `..`/absolute path, throw `AuditWriteBoundaryError(boundary.label, <offendingPaths>)` and write ZERO files (no partial governance landing). A path outside cocoder/** is REFUSED, never silently dropped and never written-and-flagged.
- DETERMINISTIC: no Date.now()/new Date()/Math.random(); stable-sorted `written`; writing the same input twice yields the same on-disk result. Create parent dirs as needed (mkdir recursive).
- EMPTY: `writes: []` => no-op, returns `{ written: [] }`, creates nothing, throws nothing.
- Writes go ONLY to `repoRoot`-joined target paths; the primitive itself does no git, no network, no agentic calls.

TESTS (new drift-apply.test.ts, all green, using a tmpdir repo): (1) writes targeting cocoder/** land on disk with exact content, parent dirs created, `written` stable-sorted; (2) a write whose path is OUTSIDE the boundary (e.g. 'packages/core/src/x.ts' with scope ['cocoder/**']) throws AuditWriteBoundaryError naming that path AND leaves the disk untouched (assert NO files were created — verify the all-or-nothing guarantee by mixing one in-scope + one out-of-scope write and confirming the in-scope one was NOT written); (3) a `..`-escaping or absolute path is refused the same way; (4) empty writes => no-op; (5) determinism => same input twice => identical files + identical result.

MUST NOT BREAK / SCOPE GUARD: write ONLY under `packages/core/src/drift/**` (apply.ts), the `packages/core/src/index.ts` barrel line(s), and the new test file. Do NOT edit commit-gate/gate.ts, write-scope/partition.ts, the other drift engines, recon.ts, the runner, personas, or anything under `cocoder/**`. Do NOT wire into the run loop.

VERIFY EVIDENCE I WILL CHECK: `pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run drift-apply` passes, the FULL core suite passes (`pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run`), workspace typecheck passes (`pnpm -w run typecheck`), and the whole-tree diff stays inside the allowed paths (commit-gate, write-scope, and the other drift engines unchanged). Report exact exit codes, files changed, and a 5-7 line summary of how the refuse-not-flag + all-or-nothing + empty invariants are enforced and which existing symbols you reused.

ATOM 6 — Build the runnable Drift-audit proof harness `scripts/proof-drift-audit.mjs`, in the EXACT style of `scripts/proof-onboard-existing.mjs` (read that file first and mirror its structure: a SUITE descriptor, an INVARIANTS list mapping each invariant to REAL named test titles, runSuite() via `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core exec vitest run <files> --reporter=json --outputFile=...`, evaluate(), printTable(), and `process.exitCode = failed ? 1 : 0`).

PURPOSE: turn 'is the Drift audit's propose->ratify->apply spine actually sound?' into ONE command with a PASS/FAIL table. It MUST NOT reimplement the drift engines — it runs the REAL drift unit tests and maps each invariant row to the specific test title(s) that protect it (same discipline as proof-onboard-existing.mjs).

SUITE: pkg `@cocoder/core`, files = the five drift test files: `tests/drift-read-claims.test.ts`, `tests/drift-read-reality.test.ts`, `tests/drift-compare.test.ts`, `tests/drift-report.test.ts`, `tests/drift-apply.test.ts`.

INVARIANTS — map these five rows to the EXACT `test('...')` titles in those files. OPEN each test file and copy the titles verbatim; a mismatched title renders the row MISSING, so verify every mapping resolves to a real title (no row may be MISSING when you finish):
1. 'P1 claims are evidence-backed and refuse-on-malformed' -> the read-claims titles covering representative evidence-backed extraction, refuse-on-unreadable-required-file, and empty-governance->zero-claims.
2. 'P2 reality reuses inventoryRepo and exposes stale-reference detection' -> the read-reality titles covering wrapping the repo inventory + listing existing paths, and omitting non-existent paths so stale references are detectable, and ignored-dir exclusion.
3. 'P3 compare is non-gameable (both-sides evidence; empty inputs -> empty)' -> the compare titles covering stale-path-only-when-missing, dead-scope-glob-only-when-zero-match, and zero-findings-for-empty-claims-or-empty-reality.
4. 'P4 report is artifacts-only and never writes governance' -> the report titles covering writes-no-files/relative-paths-only and the empty-report-without-drafts case.
5. 'P5/P6 apply is cocoder/**-only, refuse-not-flag, all-or-nothing' -> the apply titles covering refuses-out-of-boundary-before-writing-anything, refuses-absolute/escaping-paths, and empty-no-op.
